methodologySummary: ATDD/TDD: write acceptance and regression coverage before production changes.
Process hardening: keep bounded repair loops and block delivery on failed gates.
Verification before completion: require fresh command evidence before success claims.

reuseAudit.planningConstraint: Reuse existing hook contract, catalog, adapter, SDK runtime, and orchestration surfaces before adding new infrastructure.

currentRepoFindings.issueState: Issue #636 has previous plan and implementation comments, prior planning/implementation PRs #643 and #696, and open refreshed plan PR #720 referenced in the issue thread. A maintainer comment says the implementation pipeline is active and work is tracked via PRs. Treat this process as a refreshed executable implementation plan, not as evidence that no prior work exists.

relatedPlanPrs.note: Initial plan plus implementation changes landed into staging.
Refreshed plan artifact update landed into staging.
Current refreshed plan branch to update rather than duplicate.

observedPartialCoverage: packages/hooks-mux/core/src/types/lifecycle.ts already includes the named canonical phases for the 13 target entries, including tool.after_failure, tool.after_batch, turn.prompt_expansion, turn.stop_failure, task.created, task.completed, team.idle, session.setup, session.instructions_loaded, message.received, model.before_request, model.after_response, and planner.before_tool_selection.
packages/hooks-mux/adapter-claude/src/mappings.ts now builds mappings from agent-catalog with validation against CANONICAL_PHASES and LIFECYCLE_SCOPES instead of accepting unknown strings by cast.
packages/hooks-mux/adapter-claude/src/normalizer.ts, renderer.ts, and claude-adapter tests reference the target Claude event names and block/additional-context behavior.
packages/hooks-mux/cli/src/__tests__/e2e/e2e-invoke.test.ts has fixture coverage for the target native event names.
observedRemainingRisks: docs/agent-stack/hooks/coverage-matrix.md and docs/agent-stack/hooks/missing-events.md still describe the events as missing, so implementation must distinguish stale documentation from real runtime gaps.
packages/agent-mux/adapters/src/claude-code/runtime-hooks/ephemeral-config.ts currently registers only PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, SessionStart, SessionEnd, and Stop.
packages/agent-mux/adapters/src/claude-code/runtime-hooks/hook-socket-server.ts currently dispatches only the same six Claude runtime hook events.
SDK and agent-platform runtime emission paths still need a concrete current-state audit before adding task/session/turn emission behavior.
currentRepoFindings.planningImplication: Execution should start with a current-state support matrix and then implement only gaps proven by code, tests, catalog data, runtime hook registration, and docs.

targetEventClassification.note: Issue #636 names 13 target entries. The concrete lifecycle/blocking list contains 10 Claude Code items; docs/agent-stack/hooks/missing-events.md adds 3 lower-priority advanced Atlas entries that must be reconciled separately from Claude runtime emission.

targetEvents.note: Event exists; add/verify blocking decision support.
Formalize existing custom coverage as a canonical hookable event.
Gemini-specific atlas gap counted as one advanced item in docs/agent-stack/hooks/missing-events.md; reconcile phase-specific behavior during contract work.
Gemini-specific atlas event; include only where hooks-mux contract is currently incomplete.

qualityGates: Reuse audit is rendered before implementation planning.
All 13 target events have reconciled canonical contracts before production code changes.
Known docs-vs-atlas conflicts are resolved or gated by maintainer breakpoint.
Regression tests cover mapping, normalization, rendering, CLI invoke behavior, and runtime emission where applicable.
Blockable events enforce block semantics; non-blocking events fail open.
Code review approves the implementation against the event contract.
